highlight.title: Netflix's Event-Driven Architecture at Scale
highlight.summary: Netflix processes billions of real-time events daily through an event-driven microservices architecture that powers its recommendations, analytics, and playback systems. This approach allows services to react asynchronously and at massive scale while maintaining the ability to operate independently, demonstrating how event-driven patterns enable both technical resilience and business agility.

resources.title: Software Architecture Handbook for Beginners
resources.why_now: Provides an accessible introduction to core architectural concepts like client-server models and modularity, perfect for curious adults wanting to understand how software systems are organized. The handbook breaks down complex topics into digestible explanations with practical examples.

resources.title: What Is a Modular Monolith?
resources.why_now: Modular monoliths are emerging as the pragmatic middle ground between traditional monoliths and microservices, especially after Google's recent research validating this approach. Senior engineers need to understand this pattern as it offers the organizational benefits of microservices without the operational complexity.

resources.title: Netflix Architecture Deep Dive - System Design Case Study
resources.why_now: Real-world architecture examples are invaluable for understanding how patterns work at scale. Netflix's evolution from monolith to event-driven microservices showcases practical implementation of multiple patterns working together to handle billions of users and events.

resources.title: Top 10 Software Architecture Patterns for 2025
resources.why_now: As we enter 2026, this comprehensive overview of current patterns helps experienced developers stay current with architectural trends. It covers event-driven architecture, microservices evolution, and emerging patterns that are shaping modern system design.

claude_take: The architecture landscape is consolidating around pragmatic patterns that balance complexity and capability. While microservices dominated the last decade, the pendulum is swinging toward modular monoliths and event-driven designs that offer cleaner boundaries without operational overhead. The real lesson from Netflix and others isn't that you need their scale, but that thoughtful modularization and loose coupling principles apply at any size.
